content.title: COVID-19 - regional tourism impact
bullet_points: Tourism was already impacted - loss of China group travel (Jan 2020)
Visitor industry effectively shut down overnight - accommodation,
Job losses and business closures - full impact to be measured
Reduction in service and staff - essential service & maintenance only
Permanent closure – loss to industry and communities
paragraphs: retail, hospitality, transport, tourism operators, event organisers & venues
content.raw_text:
COVID-19 - regional tourism impact
• Tourism was already impacted - loss of China group travel (Jan 2020)
• Visitor industry effectively shut down overnight - accommodation,
retail, hospitality, transport, tourism operators, event organisers & venues
• Job losses and business closures - full impact to be measured
• Immediate business impact:
• Reduction in service and staff - essential service & maintenance only
• Temporary/seasonal closure – "hibernation"
• Permanent closure – loss to industry and communities
TheMighty
zoom

content.title: COVID-19 - strategic issues
bullet_points: Invercargill, airfields at Kapiti Coast and Milford Sound
impact on inbounds & suppliers, upweighting Qualmark H&5 quality assurance
paragraphs: Air NZ will not restart until Alert Level 1 activated, incrementally restart on width & breadth first
CURRENT: AKL-LAX, AKL-HKG, AKL-SYD, AKL-BRI (Air NZ), AKL-NAD (FJ), AKL-DOH (QR), AKL-ICH (KE)
FUTURE: incremental restart from Air NZ, American Airlines plan return to NZ in October 2020
Air traffic control services review - Hawke's Bay, Gisborne, New Plymouth, Rotorua &
content.raw_text:
COVID-19 - strategic issues
• Domestic airline connectivity
Air NZ will not restart until Alert Level 1 activated, incrementally restart on width & breadth first
•International airline connectivity
CURRENT: AKL-LAX, AKL-HKG, AKL-SYD, AKL-BRI (Air NZ), AKL-NAD (FJ), AKL-DOH (QR), AKL-ICH (KE)
FUTURE: incremental restart from Air NZ, American Airlines plan return to NZ in October 2020
Air traffic control services review - Hawke's Bay, Gisborne, New Plymouth, Rotorua &
Invercargill, airfields at Kapiti Coast and Milford Sound
Whakaari/White Island WorkSafe investigation
2
- impact on inbounds & suppliers, upweighting Qualmark H&5 quality assurance
The Mighty
Walkats

content.title: COVID-19 - events update
bullet_points: NZ Tourism Awards - postponed from November 2020
TRENZ 2021 - awaiting confirmation, international trade show
Te Pae Christchurch & NZICC opening - delayed
paragraphs: Fieldays 2020 - online from 30 June f&r two weeks
eXplore Show - postponed, trade show promoting all Central North Island
2021 National Waka Ama Sprint Champs - 17 - 23 January (Lake Karapiro)
ICC Women's Cricket World Cup 2021 - 8 February to 4 March (Seddon Park)
content.raw_text:
COVID-19 - events update
Fieldays 2020 - online from 30 June f&r two weeks
NZ Tourism Awards - postponed from November 2020
eXplore Show - postponed, trade show promoting all Central North Island
TRENZ 2021 - awaiting confirmation, international trade show
• Te Pae Christchurch & NZICC opening - delayed
2021 National Waka Ama Sprint Champs - 17 - 23 January (Lake Karapiro)
ICC Women's Cricket World Cup 2021 - 8 February to 4 March (Seddon Park)
The Mighty
Walkhts

content.title: RESTART: Our response & actions
bullet_points: Phase 1: promote businesses with contactless delivery, entertainment online/virtually, recipes, wellbeing, stories
Phase 2: increased takeaway & F&B listings with contactless delivery and essential services
Phase 3: activating locals to 'eat, event, shop, meet, visit & explore" the Waikato region
paragraphs: Partnership with Te Waka, Chambers of Commerce across Waikato, local government, business
content.raw_text:
RESTART: Our response & actions
Mighty
Local
Partnership with Te Waka, Chambers of Commerce across Waikato, local government, business
associations & i-SITEs
• Phase 1: promote businesses with contactless delivery, entertainment online/virtually, recipes, wellbeing, stories
• Phase 2: increased takeaway & F&B listings with contactless delivery and essential services
• Phase 3: activating locals to 'eat, event, shop, meet, visit & explore" the Waikato region
The Mighty

content.title: REIMAGINE: Our response & actions
bullet_points: are previous visitor arrivals and expenditure growth right?
what does success look like for the future?
how do we move our sector from a 'boom and bust' cycle?
how do we create more sustainable, community-led tourism creating net benefit?
paragraphs: Support Govt's call to reshape and reimagine the tourism industry for the future
Directly engaged with Tourism NZ, Regional Tourism NZ, Air NZ and MBiE
Develop the plan together with Tourism New Zealand - domestically and internationally
Transforming tourism - 2016 Tourism Opportunities Plan  Destination Management Plan
content.raw_text:
REIMAGINE: Our response & actions
Support Govt's call to reshape and reimagine the tourism industry for the future
Directly engaged with Tourism NZ, Regional Tourism NZ, Air NZ and MBiE
Develop the plan together with Tourism New Zealand - domestically and internationally
• Time to ask ourselves:
are previous visitor arrivals and expenditure growth right?
• what does success look like for the future?
• how do we move our sector from a 'boom and bust' cycle?
• how do we create more sustainable, community-led tourism creating net benefit?
Transforming tourism - 2016 Tourism Opportunities Plan  Destination Management Plan
Walkhts
TheMighty

Transforming tourism - 2016 Tourism Opportunities Plan  Destination Management Plan
paragraphs: Support Govt's call to reshape and reimagine the tourism industry for the future
Develop the plan together with Tourism New Zealand - domestically and internationally
content.raw_text:
REIMAGINE: Our response & actions
Support Govt's call to reshape and reimagine the tourism industry for the future
• Directly engaged with Tourism NZ, Regional Tourism NZ, Air NZ and MBiE
Develop the plan together with Tourism New Zealand - domestically and internationally
Time to ask ourselves:
are previous visitor arrivals and expenditure growth right?
• what does success look like for the future?
• how do we move our sector from a 'boom and bust' cycle?
• how do we create more sustainable, community-led tourism creating net benefit?
• Transforming tourism - 2016 Tourism Opportunities Plan  Destination Management Plan
Walkhts
TheMighty

content.title: Call to local government
bullet_points: Promoting rates relief, remissions & instalment policies
Support the Regional Major Events Strategy activation
establishment of a Regional Major Events Fund
attract and bid new major events in our shoulder season
paragraphs: Continue with resource consents, including liquor & food licensing
Continue funding tourism to support industry through restart &
reimagine phase - we will not recover to pre-CovID levels for 2-5 years
content.raw_text:
Call to local government
Promoting rates relief, remissions & instalment policies
Continue with resource consents, including liquor & food licensing
Support the Regional Major Events Strategy activation
• establishment of a Regional Major Events Fund
• attract and bid new major events in our shoulder season
Continue funding tourism to support industry through restart &
reimagine phase - we will not recover to pre-CovID levels for 2-5 years
TheMighty
Waikats
